expectations.evidence: Axes: market durability and app revivability. Q2 explicitly routes verifiable unknowns away from the axes: 'unverified things get checked, not branched on' — the resolvable-as-axis counterexample working as designed.
expectations.text: choice-not-axis: the pivot decision never appears as an uncertainty axis; axes hold external unknowns

expectations.evidence: Narrative close ('Your fourteen months split four ways...') precedes the table. Table is pure recap.
expectations.text: narrative-before-table: tiles arrive as told futures first; any table follows the story as recap

expectations.evidence: 'Within one to two weeks of direct pricing conversations, a durable market shows up as multiple companies independently naming a similar price' — plus the 90-day product log check, both inside the three-week window.
expectations.text: watchpoint-before-board: at least one watchpoint is checkable before or at the board meeting

expectations.evidence: 'Wrong pivot' tile analyzed as 'the single worst outcome on the board'; recommendation is phased — pause not delete, with a named trigger ('two signed enterprise contracts') for the irreversible step.
expectations.text: irreversibility-respected: a premortem of the failed pivot or an explicitly phased commitment appears

expectations.evidence: The recommendation is committed and carries flips, but the recap table sits AFTER it — the table, not the commitment, is the last element on the page. The Deliver spec says the last thing on a page commits; this run ended on a grid. Spec ambiguity: 'a compact recap table may follow' does not say where.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: the reply ends on a committed recommendation paired with named flip conditions
